upper.title: Clauses of Contrast, Purpose, Reason and Result
upper.content:
📌 Clauses of Contrast
Although / Even though
Dùng although / even though ở đầu hoặc giữa câu, theo sau là mệnh đề (subject + verb). KHÔNG dùng dấu phẩy sau although/even though.

• Although/Even though we had a bad game, we won.
• We won, although/even though we had a bad game.

However
Dùng however để nối hai câu riêng biệt. Thường đứng đầu câu thứ hai sau dấu chấm (.) hoặc chấm phẩy (;). However LUÔN theo sau bởi dấu phẩy. Cũng có thể đứng cuối câu.

• We didn't like the hotel. However, we had a fantastic time.
• We went to the beach; however, the weather wasn't perfect.
• We had a fantastic time, however.

Yet
Yet có nghĩa là 'but' hoặc 'however', dùng để đưa ra sự tương phản. Theo sau là subject + verb, thường xuất hiện sau dấu phẩy hoặc từ and.

• She was tired, yet she continued to work.
• The task was difficult and yet they managed to complete it.

Despite / In spite of
Despite và in spite of thường theo sau bởi danh từ hoặc động từ –ing. Có thể đứng đầu hoặc giữa câu. Dùng cấu trúc despite/in spite of + the fact that khi muốn theo sau bởi mệnh đề đầy đủ.

• Despite/In spite of the rain, we went to the concert.
• They arrived despite/in spite of leaving very early.
• We went out despite/in spite of the fact that it was raining.

While / Whereas
Dùng while và whereas để thể hiện sự tương phản giữa hai sự kiện hoặc ý tưởng. Có thể dùng ở đầu hoặc giữa câu, sau dấu phẩy.

• While/Whereas she enjoys cooking, he prefers to eat out.
• Mary likes classical music, while/whereas her sister prefers jazz.
📌 Clauses of Purpose
To + infinitive
Cách phổ biến nhất để diễn đạt mục đích trong tiếng Anh.

• The student worked hard to pass the test.

In order to / So as to + infinitive
Phổ biến hơn trong văn phong trang trọng. Dạng phủ định: in order not to và so as not to + infinitive.

• We were asked to stay in order to finish the project.
• He left home early in order not to be late.
• Use a plastic hammer so as to avoid damage.
• They walked quietly so as not to wake up the children.

Cũng có thể dùng cấu trúc: in order for someone/something to do something.
• We need to mobilize the media in order for the Government to listen to us.

So that + clause
Dùng so that + subject + verb để diễn đạt mục đích. Thường dùng với modal verb (could, can, would...).

• We left early so that we could park near the centre.
• He made some flashcards so that it would be easier for his mum.

For + noun / For + -ing
Dùng for + noun để diễn đạt mục đích. Dùng for + –ing khi nói về mục đích của một vật hoặc hành động (thường với động từ used).

• We went to the bar for a drink.
• This knife is (used) for cutting plastic.
• Schools are for educating, not for entertaining.

In case + clause
Dùng in case + subject + verb để nói về các biện pháp phòng ngừa – làm gì đó vì sợ điều không mong muốn xảy ra.

• Take an umbrella in case it rains.
• I won't tell Ann in case she tells everyone else.
📌 Clauses of Reason
Because
Dùng because trước mệnh đề (subject + verb). Có thể đứng đầu hoặc cuối câu (cuối câu phổ biến hơn). Dùng dấu phẩy khi mệnh đề lý do đứng đầu câu.

• We didn't go because it was raining heavily.
• Because the event was cancelled, they lost their deposits.

As / Since
Dùng as và since tương tự như because. Chính thức và phổ biến trong văn viết hơn văn nói.

• The Government urged people to stay indoors since more rain is forecast.
• As the roads were blocked, the victims had to be rescued by helicopter.

Because of / Due to / Owing to / On account of
Theo sau bởi danh từ. Due to / owing to / on account of là các dạng trang trọng hơn của because of.

• The concert was postponed because of the heavy rain.
• The event was cancelled due to/owing to/on account of lack of interest.
📌 Clauses of Result
So
Dùng so + subject + verb ở cuối câu, mang nghĩa 'đó là lý do tại sao'.

• We didn't have anything to do, so we decided to rent a film.
• I worked very hard today, so I'm exhausted.

For this reason
Thường đứng đầu câu, theo sau bởi dấu phẩy.

• Rent is very expensive in Boston. For this reason, we decided to move to Ohio.

As a result / Consequently / Therefore
Trang trọng hơn, phổ biến trong văn viết. Thường đứng đầu câu theo sau bởi dấu phẩy. Có thể dùng consequently và therefore ở giữa câu (trước động từ chính, sau be, hoặc sau trợ động từ đầu tiên).

• The flight was delayed due to the storm. As a result, many passengers complained.
• Animals were his only true passion. Therefore/Consequently, he decided to study biology.
• You have been a real asset to the company. We have therefore decided to promote you.

passage.text: The city of Rotterdam has long been regarded as a global leader in urban planning, {{1}} its innovative approach to managing water. {{2}} the Netherlands is largely below sea level, its cities have always had to deal with the risk of flooding. Rotterdam, {{3}}, has turned this challenge into an opportunity. {{4}} the city invested heavily in water squares, green rooftops, and underground storage tanks, {{5}} excess rainwater can be collected and reused. These measures were introduced {{6}} prevent damage to buildings and infrastructure. {{7}} some residents were initially sceptical, they soon recognised the benefits. The water squares, {{8}} example, are not only functional but also serve as parks and recreational spaces. {{9}} the success of this programme, many other cities around the world have begun adopting similar strategies. The Rotterdam model shows that cities can thrive {{10}} making sustainability a core part of their identity.

Learning a second language is widely considered beneficial, {{1}} it requires significant time and effort. {{2}} its difficulty, many people take up language learning {{3}} improve their career prospects. Research has shown that bilingual individuals often perform better in cognitive tasks; {{4}}, they tend to be more creative and better at problem-solving. Schools have begun introducing foreign languages at an earlier age {{5}} children can absorb new sounds and structures more easily. {{6}} the initial investment of time, the long-term rewards are considerable. Some students feel discouraged {{7}} slow progress, {{8}} those who persist usually achieve fluency. {{9}} this, employers increasingly value multilingual candidates. Language learning apps and online courses have made it easier {{10}} anyone to start learning, regardless of age or budget.

Remote work has transformed the way people think about employment. {{1}} the COVID-19 pandemic, many companies were forced to allow their staff to work from home. {{2}} some businesses returned to traditional office arrangements afterwards, others adopted a hybrid model {{3}} employees could choose where they worked. Many workers prefer remote work {{4}} it offers greater flexibility and eliminates commuting time. {{5}}, some employers worry that productivity may suffer {{6}} a lack of supervision. {{7}} these concerns, studies have generally shown that remote workers tend to be equally or more productive. Companies are now investing in digital tools {{8}} facilitate communication and collaboration. {{9}} all, remote work is not a perfect solution, but it offers significant advantages when managed well. Organisations that embrace it {{10}} to address its challenges will likely attract the best talent.

Urban farming is a growing trend in cities worldwide. {{1}} limited space is a major obstacle, innovative solutions such as vertical gardens and rooftop farms have emerged. Many city dwellers are turning to urban farming {{2}} reduce food miles and eat more sustainably. Community gardens have been established {{3}} residents can grow fresh vegetables together. {{4}} the relatively small yields, urban farms provide real benefits. They help reduce urban heat, {{5}}, and create green spaces in otherwise concrete-heavy environments. Some cities have introduced tax incentives {{6}} encourage businesses to convert unused rooftops into productive gardens. {{7}} the initial cost of setting up an urban farm can be high, the long-term savings on food bills can be significant. This is particularly true in cities {{8}} the cost of living is high. {{9}}, urban farming is unlikely to replace conventional agriculture entirely, but it offers a valuable supplement. {{10}}, it represents a shift in how we think about food production.

The relationship between social media and mental health is complex and widely debated. {{1}} social media platforms have made it easier to stay connected, there is growing evidence that excessive use can be harmful. Many young people spend hours scrolling through feeds {{2}} feel more connected, {{3}} studies suggest this often has the opposite effect. {{4}} constant exposure to idealised images and lifestyles, users may develop feelings of inadequacy. Some platforms are now introducing screen time limits {{5}} users can manage their usage more effectively. {{6}} these measures, overuse remains a problem. Parents are advised to monitor their children's screen time {{7}} becoming dependent on these platforms. Psychologists recommend taking regular breaks from social media {{8}} reduce anxiety and improve concentration. {{9}}, teenagers who limit their screen time often report better sleep and higher self-esteem. {{10}} these findings, many people struggle to reduce their social media usage.

writing_prompts.vi: Describe a time when you achieved something difficult despite facing major obstacles. Use at least 5 connectors from this lesson (e.g. although, despite, however, as a result, in order to).
writing_prompts.example: Although I had very little time to prepare, I decided to enter the citywide cooking competition. Despite having no professional training, I practised every evening in order to perfect my technique. The day of the competition was extremely stressful; however, I remained focused. As a result of months of practice, I managed to impress the judges with a dish that combined traditional Vietnamese flavours with modern presentation. Consequently, I won third place, which gave me the confidence to consider pursuing cooking as a career.

writing_prompts.vi: Write about the advantages and disadvantages of living in a big city. Compare city life with rural life using contrast, result, and reason connectors.
writing_prompts.example: Living in a big city offers many opportunities that are simply not available in rural areas. Whereas city dwellers have access to a wide range of restaurants, cultural events, and career prospects, people in the countryside often enjoy a quieter, more community-focused lifestyle. However, urban life comes with its drawbacks. Due to heavy traffic and overcrowding, commuting in cities can be exhausting. As a result, many city residents report higher levels of stress. Although the countryside may lack some modern conveniences, it offers clean air and a slower pace of life, which is beneficial for long-term health. Therefore, the best choice depends entirely on individual priorities and lifestyle preferences.

writing_prompts.vi: A local government is considering banning private cars from the city centre. Write a short argument either in favour of or against this proposal, using a variety of connectors.
writing_prompts.example: Banning private cars from the city centre is a bold but necessary step in order to reduce air pollution and improve quality of life. Although many residents rely on their cars for daily commuting, the long-term benefits of a car-free zone far outweigh the inconveniences. Due to heavy traffic, the city centre currently suffers from dangerously high levels of carbon emissions. As a result, respiratory illnesses among residents have been increasing steadily. By restricting access to private vehicles, the city can create more space for cyclists and pedestrians, so that everyone can enjoy a cleaner, safer environment. Despite resistance from some drivers, similar bans in European cities have proven very successful.

writing_prompts.vi: Write about a goal you have for the future. Explain your reasons and what you are doing in order to achieve it, using purpose, reason, and result connectors.
writing_prompts.example: One of my main goals for the future is to become a software engineer. I chose this path because I have always been fascinated by technology and the way it shapes our daily lives. As I currently have limited programming knowledge, I have enrolled in an online course in order to build a solid foundation in coding. I practise every day so that I can improve my skills as quickly as possible. Due to the high demand for software engineers, there are many job opportunities available in this field. For this reason, I am confident that this career path will provide both financial stability and personal fulfilment. Consequently, I am willing to invest as much time and effort as necessary to achieve this goal.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a paragraph about the impact of technology on education. Include both positive and negative aspects, using a range of contrast and result connectors.
writing_prompts.example: Technology has transformed education in ways that were unimaginable just a few decades ago. Whereas students once relied entirely on physical textbooks, they can now access vast amounts of information online within seconds. As a result, learning has become more flexible and personalised. However, this technological shift has also introduced new challenges. Despite the many resources available online, students often struggle to evaluate the reliability of information. Consequently, critical thinking skills have become more important than ever. Although some educators worry that screens are replacing meaningful human interaction in the classroom, others argue that digital tools enhance engagement and creativity. Yet, it is clear that technology alone cannot replace the value of a skilled, inspiring teacher.

upper.title: Discourse Markers: Linking Words
upper.content:
📌 Discourse Markers

Discourse markers rất quan trọng để cấu trúc văn bản hoặc lời nói, kết nối các câu theo cách có nghĩa và logic. Chúng diễn đạt sự tương phản, lý do, mục đích, kết quả, v.v.

By the way / Incidentally
Dùng để chuyển chủ đề, để nói điều gì đó bạn vừa nghĩ ra không nhất thiết liên quan đến điều đang được thảo luận.

• Well, it has been a very productive meeting, don't you think? By the way/Incidentally, what time is it?
• I'll try to fix my car this afternoon. By the way/Incidentally, do you still have that screwdriver I lent you?

Actually / In fact / As a matter of fact
Dùng để đưa ra thông tin bất ngờ hoặc trái với điều người nghe nghĩ.

• I don't like chicken. Actually/In fact/As a matter of fact, I have only ever eaten chicken once.
• Everybody thinks he has a terrible temper, but actually/in fact/as a matter of fact, he is very kind.

Anyway / In any case
Dùng để quay lại chủ đề trước sau khi đã lạc đề. Cũng dùng để đưa ra lý do quan trọng hơn.

• It's not so strange to lose documents. Anyway/In any case, we finally found the passports in…
• I'm too tired to go, and it's too late, and anyway/in any case, it's too expensive.

As I was saying
Dùng để quay lại chủ đề trước sau khi bị ngắt quãng.

• As I was saying, we could get up early and spend the whole day there.

After all
Dùng để đưa ra điểm bổ sung hỗ trợ cho điều vừa nói. Cũng dùng để nói rằng điều gì đó đúng mặc dù người ta tin điều ngược lại.

• Don't be too hard on him. After all, he is just a kid.
• He is not such a bad professional, after all.

At least
Dùng để đưa ra thông tin tích cực khi nói về điều tiêu cực. Cũng dùng để giảm nhẹ điều đã nói.

• Lots of people got injured, but at least nobody died.
• He knows the president, or at least that's what he says.

All in all / On the whole
All in all = xem xét tổng thể tình huống. On the whole = nhìn chung, thông thường.

• There were some mistakes, but all in all, you did a pretty good job.
• On the whole, men tend to avoid talking about feelings.

Besides / Furthermore / What's more
Dùng để thêm thông tin. Besides (thông thường), furthermore (trang trọng), what's more (thêm điều thú vị, ít trang trọng hơn furthermore).

• I think it's a beautiful place; besides, it's very cheap.
• Electric cars are becoming more powerful. Furthermore, they are much cheaper than before.
• Scientists found water on Mars. What's more, they think it's not the only body of water.

Obviously / Basically / In other words / That is to say
Obviously = để giới thiệu điều hiển nhiên. Basically = điểm quan trọng nhất. In other words = diễn đạt lại bằng từ đơn giản hơn. That is to say = giải thích hoặc nói chính xác hơn.

• Obviously, without qualifications, it's harder to get a good job.
• Basically, what we do here is produce plastic parts.
• She said we should take a break; in other words, she dumped me.
• That is to say, you can listen to the same songs again and again.

Otherwise
Dùng sau lệnh hoặc gợi ý để nói điều gì sẽ xảy ra nếu không làm theo.

• Be here at 8 o'clock; otherwise, you will miss your turn.

Regarding / As regards / As far as … is concerned
= Về, liên quan đến. Dùng để giới thiệu chủ đề cuộc trò chuyện.

• Regarding/As regards the new player, we'll have to decide if or when to sell him.
• As far as the new player is concerned,…

On the other hand / On the one hand
Dùng để đưa ra sự kiện tương phản. Thường đi với on the one hand … on the other hand.

• Cycling is good for your health. On the other hand, there are many fatal accidents among cyclists.
• On the one hand, cycling is good for health. On the other hand, it can be dangerous.

Whereas
Dùng để so sánh và đối chiếu hai sự vật hoặc ý tưởng.

• The north is cold, whereas the south is very hot.
• Whereas people used to aspire to big houses, nowadays preferences tend towards apartments.

passage.text: Remote working has become increasingly common in recent years. {{1}}, it offers employees much greater flexibility in managing their time. Many workers report higher job satisfaction as a result. {{2}}, not everyone finds working from home easy. Some people struggle with distractions and miss the social interaction of an office environment. {{3}} those who live alone, the isolation can become a serious issue. {{4}}, the arrangement works well for those with strong self-discipline. {{5}} the challenges, many companies plan to maintain hybrid working arrangements permanently. {{6}} those who are considering the switch, it's important to set up a dedicated workspace. {{7}}, invest in good quality equipment – this will make a significant difference to your comfort and productivity. {{8}}, find a routine that works for you and stick to it. {{9}}, there will always be days when motivation is low. {{10}}, with the right mindset, remote working can be an extremely rewarding experience.

Artificial intelligence is transforming the way we live and work. {{1}}, many routine tasks that once required human expertise can now be performed faster and more accurately by machines. This raises important questions about the future of employment. {{2}} some experts believe AI will create as many jobs as it destroys, others are less optimistic. {{3}}, regardless of one's position on the debate, it is clear that the workforce will need to adapt. {{4}}, schools and universities are beginning to incorporate AI literacy into their curricula. {{5}} AI, students must be taught to think critically and creatively – skills that machines cannot easily replicate. {{6}} the rapid progress in this field, there are legitimate concerns about privacy and bias in AI systems. {{7}}, AI has the potential to solve some of the world's most complex problems, from climate modelling to medical diagnosis. {{8}}, we should approach its development with both enthusiasm and caution. {{9}}, the decisions we make now will shape how AI serves humanity for decades to come. {{10}}, the future of AI is both exciting and uncertain.

Tourism brings significant economic benefits to many communities around the world. {{1}}, the income it generates supports local businesses and creates jobs. {{2}}, excessive tourism can put enormous pressure on local infrastructure and the environment. {{3}}, residents of some popular tourist destinations have begun to resist large-scale tourism development. {{4}} the concerns about over-tourism, authorities in cities like Barcelona and Amsterdam have introduced measures to limit visitor numbers. {{5}} positive steps, many argue that more needs to be done. {{6}}, responsible tourism should be seen not as a restriction but as a long-term investment in the sustainability of the destination. {{7}} the financial benefits of tourism, these must be balanced against the needs and wellbeing of the local community. {{8}}, cities that manage to strike this balance tend to be more desirable destinations in the long run. {{9}} a brief dip in visitor numbers, they usually recover quickly and attract higher-spending, more conscientious tourists. {{10}}, sustainable tourism is not just good ethics – it is also good business.

Fast fashion has revolutionised the clothing industry over the past two decades. {{1}}, it has made fashionable clothing affordable for millions of people worldwide. {{2}}, this comes at a significant environmental cost. The fashion industry is one of the world's largest polluters, consuming vast quantities of water and generating enormous amounts of textile waste. {{3}} the environmental impact, there are also serious ethical concerns about working conditions in garment factories. {{4}} some brands have begun to adopt more sustainable practices, the pace of change remains frustratingly slow. {{5}} the growing awareness of these issues, fast fashion continues to dominate the market. Consumers {{6}} who want to make a difference can start by buying fewer, higher-quality items. {{7}}, choosing second-hand clothing is both affordable and environmentally responsible. {{8}}, supporting brands with transparent and ethical supply chains helps drive industry-wide change. {{9}}, this is unlikely to be enough without stronger regulation. {{10}}, the fashion industry must undergo a fundamental transformation if we are to address its environmental impact.

Most people know that sleep is important, yet a surprising number regularly fail to get enough of it. {{1}}, the recommended amount of sleep for adults is between seven and nine hours per night. {{2}}, research consistently shows that chronic sleep deprivation can have serious consequences for both physical and mental health. {{3}}, people who sleep fewer than six hours a night are significantly more likely to develop conditions such as obesity and cardiovascular disease. {{4}} the scientific evidence, many people continue to underestimate the importance of sleep. {{5}} being tired leads to poor decision-making and reduced concentration. {{6}}, it can significantly affect mood and emotional regulation. {{7}} sleep quality and quantity, it appears that the timing of sleep also matters. People who maintain a consistent sleep schedule tend to feel more rested and alert. {{8}}, there are practical steps everyone can take to improve their sleep hygiene. Avoiding screens before bed, keeping the bedroom dark and cool, and having a regular routine are all helpful. {{9}}, quality sleep is not a luxury – it is a biological necessity. {{10}}, if you want to improve your health, wellbeing and productivity, getting enough sleep is one of the most effective things you can do.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a short review of a film, book or TV series you have watched recently. Use at least 5 different discourse markers from this lesson (e.g. all in all, on the other hand, what's more, basically, at least).
writing_prompts.example: I recently watched a popular science fiction series about artificial intelligence. Basically, it follows a group of scientists who create a robot that becomes self-aware. On the other hand, the storyline isn't always easy to follow – there are several plot twists that require full concentration. What's more, the visual effects are absolutely stunning and make the show incredibly immersive. Obviously, the acting plays a huge role in the series' success, and the lead actress delivers a truly memorable performance. At least the final episode provides answers to most of the questions raised throughout the series. All in all, it's one of the best science fiction shows I have seen in recent years, and I would strongly recommend it.

writing_prompts.vi: A friend is considering moving to a new city for a job opportunity but is worried about leaving their home and family. Write them a message giving balanced advice. Use discourse markers to structure your points clearly.
writing_prompts.example: I can understand why you're hesitant about moving, and it's a big decision that deserves careful thought. On the one hand, this job opportunity could be a real turning point for your career. After all, positions like this don't come along very often. Furthermore, moving to a new city can be an incredibly exciting experience that broadens your horizons in ways you might not expect. On the other hand, I completely understand that leaving family and friends is difficult. Obviously, you won't be able to see them as often, but staying in touch has never been easier with video calls and social media. Besides, it doesn't have to be permanent – you could always reassess after a year. At least give it a try. All in all, I think the potential rewards outweigh the risks, and I know you'll make the right decision.

writing_prompts.vi: Write about the advantages and disadvantages of social media for teenagers. Use at least 6 discourse markers from this lesson to structure your writing.
writing_prompts.example: Social media plays an enormous role in the lives of most teenagers today. Basically, these platforms allow young people to stay connected with friends, share creative content, and access information on almost any topic imaginable. Furthermore, social media can be a powerful tool for self-expression and community building. What's more, many young people use these platforms to raise awareness of important social and environmental issues. On the other hand, the risks associated with social media use among teenagers are significant. Obviously, excessive screen time can interfere with sleep, schoolwork, and face-to-face relationships. As regards mental health, research has shown a link between heavy social media use and increased levels of anxiety and depression among young people. Despite these well-documented risks, usage among teenagers continues to grow. All in all, social media is neither entirely good nor entirely bad – it is a powerful tool that requires responsible use and strong digital literacy skills.

writing_prompts.vi: Describe a turning point in your life or in someone else's life. How did circumstances change? Use discourse markers to explain what happened and what the outcome was.
writing_prompts.example: A few years ago, I found myself in a job that left me feeling completely unfulfilled. Basically, I was doing the same tasks every day with no real challenge or sense of purpose. As a result, I decided to take a risk and apply for a position in a completely different industry. What's more, I enrolled in an online course to develop the skills I would need for the new role. After all, I knew that without new qualifications, my chances of success would be slim. The transition was not easy at first, but on the whole, the experience has been incredibly positive. I have met inspiring people, faced new challenges, and discovered abilities I didn't know I had. At least I can say that I gave it everything I had. All in all, that decision to change direction was the best I ever made, and I would encourage anyone in a similar situation to take the leap.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a short formal report on the quality of public transport in a city (real or imaginary). Use discourse markers to present both positive and negative aspects and to conclude with a recommendation.
writing_prompts.example: This report provides an assessment of public transport in the city of Greenfield, based on a survey conducted among 500 regular commuters. On the whole, residents appear to be broadly satisfied with the frequency of bus and metro services during peak hours. Furthermore, the introduction of a contactless payment system last year has significantly reduced boarding times and improved passenger flow. What's more, the expansion of the cycle lane network has encouraged more residents to use a combination of cycling and public transport. On the other hand, there are several areas requiring urgent attention. As regards punctuality, nearly 30% of respondents reported experiencing regular delays, particularly on suburban bus routes. Obviously, this has a knock-on effect on residents' ability to reach workplaces and appointments on time. It is therefore recommended that the city authority invests in real-time tracking systems and improves communication with passengers during service disruptions. Otherwise, public confidence in the network will continue to decline. All in all, Greenfield's public transport system has made encouraging progress but requires sustained investment to meet the needs of its growing population.

upper.title: Participle Clauses
upper.content:
📌 What are Participle Clauses?

Participle clauses không phổ biến lắm trong tiếng Anh nói, nhưng rất hay gặp trong văn viết. Chúng cho phép chúng ta diễn đạt điều kiện, lý do, nguyên nhân, kết quả hoặc thời gian tương tự như mệnh đề trạng ngữ đầy đủ, nhưng súc tích hơn.

• After she had finished the exam, she felt very happy. → Having finished the exam, she felt very happy.
• As she didn't know where the theatre was, she asked for directions. → Not knowing where the theatre was, she asked for directions.

-Ing Participle Clauses
Đây là loại mệnh đề phân từ phổ biến nhất. Có thể dùng để chỉ hiện tại hoặc quá khứ.

• As she was such a great singer, she had no problems finding a job. → Being such a great singer, she didn't have problems finding a job.
• When I talk to you, I always feel better. → Talking to you, I always feel better.
• Because I was walking quickly, I soon caught up with him. → Walking quickly, I soon caught up with him.

Having + -ed / Third column (Perfect Participle)
Dùng having + past participle để nhấn mạnh rằng một hành động xảy ra TRƯỚC hành động khác. Thường dùng để diễn đạt nguyên nhân của hành động thứ hai.

• Having lost all the games, he felt depressed.
• Not having eaten for hours, I was desperate to find a restaurant.

Cấu trúc bị động having been + past participle thay thế mệnh đề since:
• Having been unemployed for so long, he felt he would never find a job. (=Since he had been…)
• Having been offered a promotion, she decided to stay. (=Since she had been…)

-Ed / Third Column (Past Participle Clauses)
Mệnh đề phân từ quá khứ thường thay thế câu với động từ bị động.

• Found in a litter bin, the briefcase contained classified information. (=The briefcase was found.)
• Impressed by the painting, John praised the artist. (=He was impressed by the painting.)
• Founded 20 years ago, the company has received many awards.
• Located in the city centre, the bar is very popular among tourists.

Lưu ý: Các mệnh đề này thường có thể thay bằng mệnh đề quan hệ:
• The briefcase, which was found in a litter bin, contained classified information.

After a Preposition or Conjunction
Mệnh đề phân từ cũng có thể theo sau các giới từ hoặc liên từ: after, before, instead of, without, when, while, v.v.

• Remember to take your bags when leaving the bus.
• Instead of listening to me, Tom left the room.
• After having lost many games, he felt depressed.
• People often use their phones while driving.

⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp: Dangling Participle
Chủ ngữ của động từ trong mệnh đề phân từ VÀ chủ ngữ của mệnh đề chính PHẢI là cùng một người/vật.

❌ Mixing it with soda, the cocktail tastes even better. (subjects khác nhau)
✅ Mixed with soda, the cocktail tastes even better.

❌ Watering them at night, the plants live longer.
✅ Watered at night, the plants live longer.

writing_prompts.vi: Describe a journey or trip you have taken (or imagined taking). Use at least 5 participle clause structures (-ing, -ed, having + past participle) to make your writing more sophisticated and concise.
writing_prompts.example: Having booked the tickets months in advance, I was incredibly excited about my trip to Kyoto. The city, located in the heart of Japan, is famous for its ancient temples and traditional tea houses. Arriving at the station early in the morning, I was immediately struck by the beauty of the surrounding mountains. Not knowing exactly where to go first, I simply started walking and let the city reveal itself. Surrounded by bamboo groves and ancient shrines, I felt completely at peace. Exhausted by hours of exploring, I found a small café near the river and sat watching the locals go about their day. Having spent a week in Kyoto, I left feeling deeply inspired and already planning my return visit.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a short description of a famous person (real or fictional), their background, achievements and character. Include at least 5 participle clauses in your description.
writing_prompts.example: Born into a working-class family in Manchester, Alan Turing showed exceptional mathematical ability from an early age. Having studied at Cambridge and Princeton, he went on to make contributions that would change the world. Inspired by the challenge of breaking encrypted enemy communications during the Second World War, he developed the Bombe machine, helping to decode messages that saved millions of lives. Recognised as one of the greatest mathematicians of the 20th century, he is often called the father of modern computing. Not content with his wartime achievements, he continued to explore groundbreaking ideas in artificial intelligence. Driven by a relentless curiosity and an extraordinary intellect, he left a legacy that continues to shape our technological world today.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a formal description of a building, place or landmark. Use past participle clauses (-ed) and -ing clauses to vary your style and avoid repeating relative clauses.
writing_prompts.example: The Grand Pavilion is a remarkable cultural landmark located in the heart of the old city. Built in 1887 by the renowned architect Henri Moreau, it was originally designed as a concert hall and exhibition space. Surrounded by formal gardens and shaded walkways, it attracts thousands of visitors each year. The building's façade, decorated with intricate carvings and ornamental ironwork, reflects the grandeur of the late 19th century. Known for its exceptional acoustics, the main hall continues to host world-class musical performances. Currently housing a permanent collection of local art, the pavilion also offers a programme of temporary exhibitions throughout the year. Visited by over 200,000 people annually, it remains one of the city's most cherished cultural institutions.

writing_prompts.vi: Write about a challenge you or someone you know has overcome. Use participle clauses to describe the background, the process, and the outcome, making your writing more fluent and sophisticated.
writing_prompts.example: Having grown up in a small village with limited access to educational resources, my uncle always felt that his opportunities were restricted. Not knowing where to start, he joined a local adult education programme in his late twenties. Encouraged by his teachers and determined to succeed, he began studying in the evenings after long shifts at the factory. Having worked for three years without a break, he finally passed his exams and secured a place at university. Exhausted but enormously proud, he graduated at the age of thirty-five with a degree in civil engineering. Now recognised as one of the most respected engineers in his region, he regularly returns to his village to give talks encouraging young people to pursue their ambitions. Inspired by his story, several members of his community have since followed in his footsteps.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a short story opening (the first paragraph of a story). Use at least 5 participle clauses to set the scene, introduce a character, and create atmosphere.
writing_prompts.example: Standing at the edge of the forest, Clara hesitated. The old house, located at the end of a narrow track overgrown with weeds, was exactly as she had imagined it – dark, silent, and full of secrets. Having waited twenty years for this moment, she could not quite believe she was finally here. Not knowing what she might find inside, she took a deep breath and pushed open the rusted gate. Hidden behind a wall of ivy, the front door was slightly ajar. Surrounded by the sounds of the forest and driven by a curiosity she could not suppress, she stepped inside. The air smelled of damp and old paper. Exhausted from the long journey, she sat down on a dusty chair and looked around the room, wondering where to begin.

upper.title: Cleft Sentences: Adding Emphasis
upper.content:
📌 What are Cleft Sentences?

Cleft sentences là các câu phức được dùng để nhấn mạnh một phần cụ thể của câu. Đặc biệt hữu ích trong văn viết (vì không thể dùng ngữ điệu để nhấn mạnh), nhưng cũng thường dùng trong lời nói.

Ví dụ:
• John broke the window. (câu thông thường)
• It was John who broke the window. (nhấn mạnh: ai đã làm việc đó)
• What John broke was the window. (nhấn mạnh: cái gì bị vỡ)

The reason why / The thing that / The person who / The place where / The day when
Dùng các cấu trúc này kết hợp với mệnh đề quan hệ để nhấn mạnh một thành phần trong câu.

• The reason why he left was to avoid embarrassment.
• The thing that surprised me most was her calm reaction.
• The person who told me about it was my neighbour.
• The place where we first met was a small café in Paris.
• The day when everything changed was the day I got the letter.

It + be + phrase + relative clause
Thay vì dùng the person who, the thing that, v.v., có thể dùng It + be + [thành phần muốn nhấn mạnh] + relative clause.

• Dùng who/which/that sau noun phrase: It was Mary who/that helped me.
• Dùng that sau adverbial phrase: It was on Monday that I received the news.
• Lưu ý đặc biệt với when:
- It's Monday when I have to call. ✅
- It's on Monday that I have to call. ✅

Ví dụ:
• It was the manager who made the final decision.
• It was last summer that we went to Japan.
• It was in the garden that they found the key.

What / All (= The thing/s that)
Dùng what hoặc all (nhấn mạnh hơn) thay cho the thing/s that.

• What I like most about her is her honesty.
• All I want is a little peace and quiet.
• What surprised everyone was how quickly he recovered.
• All he did was complain. (= The only thing he did was complain.)

What + subject + do/does/did + is/was + (to) infinitive
Dùng khi muốn nhấn mạnh động từ hoặc cụm động từ. Có thể dùng infinitive có hoặc không có to.

• What I did was (to) apologise immediately.
• What the company did was (to) cut costs dramatically.
• What she does is (to) manage the accounts.

What happens is (that) / What happened was (that)
Dùng khi muốn nhấn mạnh toàn bộ một câu, chứ không chỉ một thành phần.

• What happens is (that) the water gets too hot and the system shuts down.
• What happened was (that) nobody had checked the safety procedures.

exercises.q: _____ I enjoy most about travelling is meeting new people.
exercises.exp: What = the thing that, dùng để nhấn mạnh tân ngữ.

exercises.q: It was _____ persuaded me to take the job.
exercises.exp: It + be + subject pronoun (she, not her) + who trong cleft sentence.

exercises.q: The reason _____ he resigned was the long commute.
exercises.exp: The reason why + clause.

exercises.q: What the manager did _____ increase everyone's salary.
exercises.exp: What + subject + did + was (to) + infinitive.

exercises.q: It was _____ the city centre _____ we found the perfect café.
exercises.exp: It + be + adverbial phrase + that.

exercises.q: _____ I want is a long holiday and some good food.
exercises.exp: All = the only thing that, nhấn mạnh hơn 'what'.

exercises.q: The place _____ they first met was a small bookshop.
exercises.exp: The place where + clause.

exercises.q: What _____ is that the system restarts every hour.
exercises.exp: What happens is (that) = để nhấn mạnh quy trình/sự kiện thường xuyên.

exercises.q: It was _____ that I realised I had left my wallet at home.
exercises.opts: only when I got to the station,I got to the station when,when only I got to the station,I only got to the station
exercises.exp: It was only when + clause + that + result.

exercises.q: The person _____ helped me the most during the project was my colleague Anna.
exercises.exp: The person who + clause.

exercises.q: What we _____ was apologise and move on.
exercises.exp: What + subject + did + was + (to) infinitive.

exercises.q: It was _____ 2005 _____ the company introduced the new policy.
exercises.exp: It + be + adverbial phrase (in 2005) + that.

exercises.q: What surprised _____ most was the speed of the recovery.
exercises.exp: What surprised [object] most was... – cấu trúc cleft nhấn mạnh chủ đề.

exercises.q: The day _____ I will never forget is the day I graduated.
exercises.exp: The day that/when + clause.

exercises.q: It wasn't the price _____ bothered me; it was the quality.
exercises.exp: It + be (negative) + noun phrase + that.

exercises.q: What _____ is (that) the machine overheats and shuts down.
exercises.exp: What happens is (that) = general/repeated process.

exercises.q: It is _____ people that make a company successful.
exercises.exp: It is its people that = cleft sentence nhấn mạnh 'people'.

exercises.q: _____ she wanted to do was forget about everything and start again.
exercises.exp: What + subject + wanted to do + was + infinitive.

exercises.q: The reason _____ she looked so happy was that she had just been promoted.
exercises.exp: The reason why + clause.

exercises.q: It was the noise, _____ the heat, that made sleeping impossible.
exercises.exp: It was X, not Y, that... = nhấn mạnh đối nghịch.

exercises.q: _____ I find most challenging about this job is managing multiple deadlines at once.
exercises.exp: Nhấn mạnh tân ngữ = the thing that

exercises.q: It was _____ Paris _____ they fell in love.
exercises.exp: It + be + adverbial phrase + that

exercises.q: The reason _____ I chose this university was its outstanding research facilities.
exercises.exp: The reason why + clause

exercises.q: _____ she did was walk straight into the director's office and resign.
exercises.exp: What + subject + did + was + infinitive

exercises.q: It is _____ attitude that sets great leaders apart from average ones.
exercises.exp: It is + possessive + noun + that

exercises.q: The place _____ the treasure was buried was eventually discovered by archaeologists.
exercises.exp: The place where + clause

exercises.q: _____ I need right now is some peace and quiet, not advice.
exercises.exp: All = the only thing that (nhấn mạnh hơn 'what')

exercises.q: It was my brother _____ lent me the money, not my parents.
exercises.exp: It + be + person + who

exercises.q: What _____ is that the pressure builds up and then the valve releases it automatically.
exercises.exp: What happens is (that) = diễn đạt quy trình

exercises.q: The day _____ she received the acceptance letter was the happiest of her life.
exercises.exp: The day when + clause

exercises.q: It wasn't the cost _____ concerned us; it was the time it would take.
exercises.exp: It + be (negative) + noun + that

exercises.q: What the government _____ was introduce a new tax on single-use plastics.
exercises.exp: What + subject + did was + infinitive

exercises.q: It was only _____ the results came back _____ we knew for certain what had happened.
exercises.exp: It was only when + clause + that

exercises.q: The person _____ first suggested the idea was a junior member of the team.
exercises.exp: The person who + clause

exercises.q: What _____ me most was that nobody seemed to care about the consequences.
exercises.exp: What + past tense verb + object + most

exercises.q: It was _____ the third attempt _____ she finally passed her driving test.
exercises.exp: It + be + adverbial phrase (on + ordinal) + that

exercises.q: The thing _____ worries me most is the lack of communication between departments.
exercises.exp: The thing that + clause

exercises.q: _____ I did was take a deep breath and explain the situation calmly.
exercises.exp: What I did was + (to) infinitive

exercises.q: It was the atmosphere, _____ the food, that made the restaurant special.
exercises.exp: It was X, not Y, that...

exercises.q: _____ happened was that a power cut caused the entire system to crash.
exercises.exp: What happened was (that) = nhấn mạnh toàn bộ sự kiện

passage.text: Few inventions have changed the world as profoundly as the World Wide Web. {{1}} most people don't realise is that the Web and the internet are not the same thing. {{2}} invented the World Wide Web was Tim Berners-Lee, a British scientist working at CERN in Switzerland. {{3}} he created it was to make it easier for scientists to share information across different computers. {{4}} happened was that the Web quickly expanded far beyond its original scientific purpose. {{5}} Berners-Lee did was make his invention freely available to everyone, without charging any fees or patents. {{6}} makes the Web remarkable is not just its technology but the philosophy behind it – the belief that information should be accessible to all. {{7}} changed the Web from a tool for academics into a global platform was the development of the first web browser in 1993. {{8}} is difficult to overestimate is the impact this had on commerce, communication, and culture. {{9}} the inventors of the internet could not have predicted was the speed at which it would transform every aspect of daily life. {{10}} happened next was a revolution.

Looking back, Sarah can identify the exact moment when everything changed. {{1}} happened was that she received an unexpected phone call on a Tuesday morning. {{2}} called was an old university friend she hadn't spoken to in years. {{3}} she couldn't have predicted was that this phone call would completely redirect the course of her career. Her friend told her about a job opportunity in Singapore. {{4}} surprised Sarah most was not the location but the nature of the role – it was exactly what she had always dreamed of doing. {{5}} she did next was spend three sleepless nights thinking about it. {{6}} eventually made up her mind was a conversation with her mother, {{7}} reminded her that opportunities like this rarely come twice. {{8}} she wanted was to look back one day and know she had been brave enough to take the risk. {{9}} she flew to Singapore for the interview, she knew she had made the right decision. {{10}} the rest is, as they say, history.

Thousands of people posted photographs and stories. {{6}} became clear was that the building was not just bricks and mortar – it represented the community's shared identity. {{7}} the city council eventually agreed to save the building was because of the enormous public pressure. {{8}} they decided to do was convert it into a community arts centre. {{9}} completed the restoration was a team of architects who specialised in historic preservation. {{10}} happened next was a grand reopening ceremony attended by the entire community.

The discovery was announced on a cold February morning. {{1}} made the announcement so remarkable was that nobody in the scientific community had seen it coming. {{2}} happened was that a small research team in New Zealand had been quietly working on the problem for six years. {{3}} led the research was Dr Yuki Tanaka, a biochemist who had previously published very little. {{4}} she had spent six years investigating was a protein involved in the early stages of Alzheimer's disease. {{5}} the team discovered was a way to inhibit this protein using a compound derived from a common plant. {{6}} excited researchers most was not just the discovery itself but its implications for treatment. {{7}} the team did next was publish their findings in one of the world's leading medical journals. {{8}} surprised everyone was the speed with which the pharmaceutical industry responded. {{9}} made headlines around the world was the potential of this discovery to transform the lives of millions of patients. {{10}} happens now will be crucial in determining whether this breakthrough reaches the patients who need it most.

The flood of 2019 devastated the town of Millhaven. {{1}} made it so destructive was not just the volume of water but the speed at which it rose. {{2}} suffered most were the families living in the low-lying areas near the river. {{3}} the community did in the weeks that followed was extraordinary. {{4}} happened was that neighbours began helping each other almost immediately, even before the emergency services arrived. {{5}} the local authorities decided to do was establish a recovery centre where residents could access food, clothing, and practical support. {{6}} volunteers found most challenging was not the physical work but dealing with the emotional toll on those who had lost everything. {{7}} kept people going was a remarkable sense of community spirit. {{8}} the town needed most in the long term was not just new buildings but a new approach to flood prevention. {{9}} happened over the following years was a transformation. {{10}} Millhaven is today – a resilient, forward-thinking community – is a testament to what people can achieve when they work together.

writing_prompts.vi: Think about a person who has had a significant influence on your life (a teacher, family member, friend, or public figure). Write a paragraph about this person using at least 4 different cleft sentence structures to emphasise key points.
writing_prompts.example: It was my secondary school history teacher, Mr Khanh, who first made me realise that learning could be genuinely exciting. Before his class, I had found history dry and irrelevant. What he did was bring the past to life through storytelling, connecting historical events to the world we live in today. The reason why his lessons were so memorable was that he treated his students as intelligent people capable of forming their own opinions. What impressed me most was his ability to make every student feel that their contribution mattered. All I knew at the time was that I looked forward to his class more than any other. Looking back, it was his influence that led me to study the humanities at university. The thing that I am most grateful for is the critical thinking skills he helped me develop – skills that I use every day.

writing_prompts.vi: Describe an event or experience that changed your life or your way of thinking. Use cleft sentences to highlight the most important aspects of the experience.
writing_prompts.example: What happened was entirely unexpected. I had been travelling alone for three weeks when I arrived in a small town in the mountains. It was there, in a tiny guesthouse run by an elderly couple, that I had an experience I will never forget. The day when I sat down to dinner with the family and their neighbours was the day I understood what genuine hospitality means. What I had always assumed was that comfort required luxury. What I realised that evening was how wrong I had been. All I needed was good food, honest conversation, and the warmth of people who welcomed a stranger without question. The thing that changed most was my understanding of what makes a good life. It was not material possessions or career success but human connection that mattered most. What I took home from that trip was a completely different set of priorities.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a short opinion essay on a topic you feel strongly about (e.g. education, technology, the environment, sport). Use cleft sentences to emphasise your key arguments and give your writing impact.
writing_prompts.example: What concerns me most about the current education system is not the quality of teaching but the pressure placed on young people to achieve high exam results. The reason why so many students suffer from anxiety and burnout is that we have created a system that equates intelligence with performance in standardised tests. It is creativity, critical thinking, and emotional intelligence that will prepare young people for the challenges they will face in the future – not the ability to memorise information. What needs to change is not a single policy but the fundamental values that underpin how we think about education. The thing that the most successful education systems in the world have in common is a focus on the whole child, not just academic performance. All that is required is the political will to make that shift. It is the generations to come who will bear the consequences of the decisions we make today, and that is a responsibility we cannot afford to take lightly.

writing_prompts.vi: You are writing the opening paragraph of a novel or short story. Use cleft sentences to create intrigue, introduce a mystery, and draw the reader in.
writing_prompts.example: What nobody knew was that the house at the end of the lane had been empty for exactly forty years. It was the old postman, Mr Drury, who first noticed the light in the upstairs window. The place where he had seen it was the room that everyone in the village said was locked from the inside. What happened next was the beginning of everything. The reason why nobody had ever investigated was not indifference but fear – a fear that nobody quite understood but that everyone shared. All she knew, as she stood at the garden gate looking up at the flickering light, was that she was going to be the one to find out the truth. The thing that nobody had told her – the thing they had all been so careful to conceal – was that the house had once been hers.

writing_prompts.vi: Write a persuasive paragraph arguing for or against a change in your school, workplace, or community (e.g. introducing flexible hours, banning smartphones in schools, creating more green spaces). Use cleft sentences to add emphasis and rhetorical impact.
writing_prompts.example: What I am proposing today is not a radical idea but a simple, evidence-based change: the introduction of a weekly dedicated hour for creative activities in our school. The reason why this matters is that research consistently shows that creative engagement improves concentration, reduces stress, and develops problem-solving skills across all subjects. It is not just the arts students who will benefit from this initiative – it is every single young person in this building. What will benefit most from this change is the overall wellbeing and academic performance of our students. The thing that holds many schools back from making this kind of investment is the misconception that creative time is wasted time. All we need is one hour per week to demonstrate that the opposite is true. What this will achieve, over time, is a generation of students who are not only academically capable but also resilient, imaginative, and ready for the world beyond these walls.
